items.text: Plan an accessible public transport trip

title: Plan an Accessible Public Transport Trip
introduction: This page tells you how to plan an accessible trip on public transport.
openingParagraphs: Planning before you leave can make public transport easier to use.
You can check accessible services, travel alerts and support options.

blocks.title: Plan your trip before you leave

paragraphs.text: Use a trip planning app or website to choose where you want to go and when you want to leave.

blocks.imgAlt: A wheelchair user planning a bus trip on a phone

blocks.title: Choose accessible services

paragraphs.text: Accessible services are designed to be easier for people with disability to use. Choose services that meet your access needs.

blocks.imgAlt: A wheelchair user choosing an accessible bus or train on a phone

blocks.title: Check travel alerts

paragraphs.text: Check for travel alerts before you leave. An alert can tell you about delays, changes or lifts that are not working.

blocks.imgAlt: A wheelchair user checking a travel alert about a station lift

blocks.title: Ask for support

paragraphs.text: You can ask a friend, carer or public transport staff member to help with your trip.

blocks.imgAlt: A public transport staff member helping a wheelchair user board a train

downloads.heading: Print, download or share this page
descriptionParagraphs: Copy a link, print this page, download an EPUB or share this page with someone.

about.aboutDescription: This page uses Easy Read. Easy Read uses simple words, clear structure and meaningful pictures to make information easier to understand.
